title: License-text snapshot capture (committed copy + SHA-256 + Wayback)
context: The gate-002 eligibility checklist requires that every source license is recorded with a committed license-text snapshot (committed copy + SHA-256 + Wayback save URL) rather than a bare URL that can drift or disappear. The project needs tooling that automates: fetching the license text from a URL, computing its SHA-256, saving it to the repo under a canonical path, and recording the Wayback Machine save URL. This makes the provenance requirement for license snapshots systematically enforceable rather than a manual copy-paste step. Depends on gate-002 (which defines the requirement) and tooling-006 (which provides the validation script foundation).
objective: Implement and merge a license-text snapshot capture tool that fetches a license URL, commits a local copy, computes its SHA-256, and records a Wayback Machine save URL, automating the committed-snapshot requirement from gate-002.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool accepts a source license URL and outputs: a committed license-text file under a canonical path (e.g. licenses/<source-slug>/LICENSE.txt), its SHA-256 hash, and a Wayback Machine save URL (https://web.archive.org/save/<url>).
SHA-256 is computed over the committed file and recorded in a companion .json metadata file alongside the committed license text.
Tool is idempotent: re-running on an already-captured license URL does not overwrite the committed copy (to preserve the original snapshot), or records a version history.
Instructions for triggering a Wayback save (or automated via the Wayback Save API) are included and tested.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; no credentials committed; MIT license; commit DCO signed-off.
output: A merged PR adding the license-text snapshot capture tool to the project repo, with tests demonstrating it fetches, commits, hashes, and records a Wayback URL for a sample license.
